drain_alignment.note: Eighth drain-agreement case; drain-divergence pattern continuing to erode.

placement_alignment.note: Claude's default 2mm estimate reflects low-confidence monitor view; GPT-4o offers tighter read.

fls_score_reconciliation.notes: Tightest reconciliation in the V-series. Both teachers independently converged to within 1.6 FLS and 1.9 seconds — effectively unanimous agreement on this run.

systematic_findings.endpoint_agreement: Δtime 1.9s — strongest endpoint agreement in the series. Breaks the V18/V23 endpoint-definition drift. Suggests V24's completion frame is unambiguous.
systematic_findings.phase_boundary_disagreement: Claude bundles reinforcing manipulation into suture_cut (40s), GPT-4o bundles it into third_throw (49.1s). Net throws+cut converges within 8.4s — same phenomenon observed on V17, V19, V22. Boundary placement remains the primary source of per-phase variance even when endpoints agree.
systematic_findings.penalty_scope_disagreement: Sixth case of Claude > GPT-4o on penalties (Δ 3.5). Claude's 4.0mm placement default vs GPT-4o's 2.5mm read accounts for most of it. Recommendation to formalize the rubric's 5-penalty list stands.
systematic_findings.drain_agreement_streak: 8 agree / 6 diverge — pattern is now clearly drifting toward consistent agreement.
systematic_findings.confidence_split: GPT-4o 0.67 vs Claude 0.35 — largest confidence gap of any V-video. Claude explicitly flags monitor-recording limitations; GPT-4o appears less penalized by the same conditions.
